name: Fork Union Military Academy
about: Fork Union Military Academy is an all-male Christian college-preparatory boarding school founded in 1898 in rural Fork Union, Virginia, affiliated with the Baptist General Association of Virginia. It serves grades 7-12 plus a postgraduate year on a roughly 1,000-acre campus and is known for its 'One Subject Plan' academic schedule. The academy has a strong athletic tradition and has sent more than 100 alumni to the NFL.
notable_alumni: Vinny Testaverde, Eddie George, Plaxico Burress
financial_aid_notes: Need-based; more than half of enrolled students receive some need-based aid each year

name: Foxcroft School
about: Foxcroft School is an independent college-preparatory boarding and day school for girls in grades 9-12, founded in 1914 on a roughly 500-acre campus near Middleburg, Virginia. The school enrolls around 160 students, the large majority of whom board, and offers a college-preparatory curriculum with more than a dozen AP courses. It is known for its equestrian program alongside a range of athletics and arts.
college_matriculation: University of Virginia, College of William and Mary, College of Charleston
financial_aid_notes: Need-based; ~51% of students receive aid; merit scholarships including Ruth T. Bedford '32 Merit Scholarship for the Arts (up to $25,000)

name: Hargrave Military Academy
about: Hargrave Military Academy is an all-male college-preparatory military boarding school founded in 1909 in Chatham, Virginia, affiliated with the Baptist General Association of Virginia and emphasizing Christian values. It serves grades 7 through postgraduate and prepares students for college and the U.S. service academies. The school enrolls about 195 cadets, most of whom board.
college_matriculation: The Citadel, Virginia Tech, Norwich University
financial_aid_notes: ~60% of students receive aid; average grant ~$11,000; merit-based aid prioritized

name: Madeira School
about: The Madeira School is an independent college-preparatory boarding and day school for girls in grades 9-12, founded in 1906 by Lucy Madeira Wing on a 376-acre campus in McLean, Virginia, just outside Washington, D.C. It enrolls roughly 338 students, about half of whom board, and offers a college-preparatory curriculum including a signature off-campus Co-Curriculum internship program. The school's motto is 'Festina lente' (Make haste slowly).

name: Massanutten Military Academy
affiliation: Reformed Church in the United States
about: Massanutten Military Academy is a coeducational college-preparatory military boarding and day school founded in 1899 in Woodstock, Virginia, in the Shenandoah Valley. It serves grades 8-12 plus a postgraduate year and is one of only a small number of coeducational military schools in the country, with females making up roughly 40% of cadets. The academy operates an Army JROTC program recognized as an Honor Unit with Distinction.
religious_affiliation: Reformed Church in the United States
financial_aid_notes: Need- and achievement-based scholarships; aid averages ~15% off tuition (not available to day students, international cadets, PG basketball, or summer school)

name: The Miller School of Albemarle
about: The Miller School of Albemarle is a coeducational college-preparatory boarding and day school founded in 1878 by Samuel Miller on a roughly 1,100-acre campus near Charlottesville, Virginia. One of the first coeducational boarding schools in America, it serves grades 8-12 plus a postgraduate year and is known for programs in sustainable agriculture, engineering, and competitive cycling. The campus includes a working farm, a lake, and miles of mountain bike trails.
college_matriculation: Virginia Tech, University of Mary Washington, James Madison University, Pennsylvania State University, Boston University
financial_aid_notes: Need-based; ~46% of students receive aid; average boarding grant ~$26,400

name: Randolph-Macon Academy
about: Randolph-Macon Academy is a coeducational college-preparatory boarding and day school founded in 1892 in Front Royal, Virginia, about 70 miles west of Washington, D.C. It serves grades 8-12 on a 135-acre campus and incorporates an Air Force JROTC leadership program. The academy enrolls roughly 274 students and offers Advanced Placement and dual-enrollment courses.
notable_alumni: Walter E. Boomer, Harlan Crow, Joel Shankle
head_of_school: Brig. Gen. David C. Wesley, USAF (Ret.)

name: St. Anne's-Belfield School
about: St. Anne's-Belfield School is an independent, nonsectarian coeducational day school with an upper-school boarding program, located in Charlottesville, Virginia. Founded in 1910 and formed by the merger of two earlier schools, it serves students from pre-kindergarten through grade 12 and enrolls roughly 880-980 students across its divisions. It offers an extensive college-preparatory curriculum with nearly 40 advanced and AP courses.
college_matriculation: University of Virginia, Virginia Tech, James Madison University, University of Chicago, NYU
notable_alumni: Nat Friedman, Chris Long, Kyle Long, Schuyler Fisk, Tom Perriello
financial_aid_notes: Need-based; ~42% of students receive financial aid
